entry.title: 27. Remove Element
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Two Pointers, Time complexity: O(2*N)=O(N).

Approach 2: Two Pointers - when elements to remove are rare, Time complexity: O(N)

entry.title: 26. Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach: Two Pointers, Time complextiy : O(n)

entry.title: 25. Reverse Nodes in k-Group
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach: Iteration

Approach: Recursion

entry.title: 24. Swap Nodes in Pairs
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Iterative Approach, Time Complexity: O(N), where N is the size of the linked list

Approach 2: Recursive Approach, Time Complexity: O(N)

entry.title: 23. Merge k Sorted Lists
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Brute Force, Time complexity: O(NlogN) where NN is the total number of nodes.

Approach 2: Compare one by one, Time complexity: O(kN) where k is the number of linked lists

Approach 3: Optimize Approach 2 by Priority Queue, Time complexity: O(Nlogk) where k is the number of linked lists. link

Approach 4: Merge with Divide And Conquer, Time complexity: O(Nlogk) where k is the number of linked lists.

entry.title: 22. Generate Parentheses
entry.content:
LeetCode

Backtracking, Time Complexity: O(4^n/sqrt{n}})

link

Explanation
Approach 3: Closure Number (DP)\
First consider how to get the result f(n) from previous result f(0)…f(n-1).
Actually, the result f(n) will be put an extra () pair to f(n-1). Let the “(“ always at the first position, to produce a valid result, we can only put “)” in a way that there will be i pairs () inside the extra () and n - 1 - i pairs () outside the extra pair.

Approach 1: Iteration

Approach 2: Recursion

entry.title: 14. Longest Common Prefix
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Horizontal scanning, Time complexity: O(S) , where S is the sum of all characters in all strings

Approach 2: Vertical scanning, Time complexity: O(S) , where S is the sum of all characters in all strings


Approach 3: Divide and conquer, Time complexity: O(S), where S is the number of all characters in the array


Approach 4: Binary search, Time complexity: O(S⋅logm)

Approach 5: Prefix trie, Time complexity : O(S)

Approach 2: Two Pointer Approach
